problems.content: What is the circumference of a circle with radius 7 cm? Express in terms of π.

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Find the equation of the circle with center (3, -1) and passing through (6, 3).

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal in length.
problems.correct_answer: Place rectangle with vertices at (0,0), (a,0), (a,b), (0,b). Diagonal 1: √(a²+b²). Diagonal 2: √(a²+b²). Equal.

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Find the area of a triangle with vertices A(1,1), B(4,1), C(2,5).

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Find the center and radius of the circle x² + y² + 6x - 2y - 6 = 0.

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Prove that the medians of a triangle are concurrent (meet at a single point).
problems.correct_answer: Using coordinate geometry: place triangle at A(0,0), B(2a,0), C(2b,2c). Medians from each vertex to opposite midpoint all pass through ((2a+2b)/3, 2c/3).

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Two circles x² + y² = 25 and x² + y² - 8x + 6y + 7 = 0 intersect. Find the equation of their common chord.

problems.subject: geometry
problems.content: Find the length of the tangent from the point (7, 1) to the circle x² + y² - 4x + 6y - 12 = 0.

problems.subject: calculus
problems.content: Find the local maximum and minimum of f(x) = x³ - 6x² + 9x + 1.
problems.correct_answer: Local max at x=1: f(1)=5. Local min at x=3: f(3)=1.

problems.content: In how many ways can 5 students be arranged in a line?

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: Two dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum is 7?

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: Find the standard deviation of: 2, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 7, 9. (Population std dev)

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: From a group of 8 people, how many ways can a committee of 3 be selected?

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: A binomial experiment has n=6 trials with p=0.3. Find P(X=2).

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: Prove that the variance of the sum of two independent random variables equals the sum of their variances.
problems.correct_answer: Var(X+Y) = E[(X+Y-μ_X-μ_Y)²] = E[(X-μ_X)²] + 2E[(X-μ_X)(Y-μ_Y)] + E[(Y-μ_Y)²]. Independence gives E[(X-μ_X)(Y-μ_Y)] = 0. So Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y).

problems.subject: probability_statistics
problems.content: A test has 10 questions, each with 4 choices. A student guesses randomly on all. What is the probability of getting at least 8 correct?

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A rectangle has a perimeter of 36 cm and an area of 80 cm². Find its dimensions.

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A ball is thrown upward with initial velocity 20 m/s. Its height is h(t) = 20t - 5t². When does it reach maximum height and what is that height?

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A car travels 120 km at a certain speed. If it had traveled 20 km/h faster, the trip would have taken 1 hour less. Find the original speed.

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: Explain why the equation x² + 1 = 0 has no real solutions but has complex solutions.
problems.correct_answer: For real numbers, x² ≥ 0, so x² + 1 ≥ 1 > 0. No real x satisfies x² + 1 = 0. In complex numbers, x = ±i where i² = -1.
problems.correct_answer_latex: x^2 \geq 0 \implies x^2 + 1 > 0 \text{ for all real } x. \text{ Complex solutions: } x = \pm i

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A ladder 10 m long leans against a wall. The bottom slides away at 1 m/s. How fast is the top sliding down when the bottom is 6 m from the wall?

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: Prove that for any positive integers a and b, if ab is even, then a is even or b is even.
problems.correct_answer: Proof by contrapositive: Assume a is odd and b is odd. Then a = 2m+1 and b = 2n+1. ab = (2m+1)(2n+1) = 4mn + 2m + 2n + 1 = 2(2mn+m+n) + 1, which is odd. So if ab is even, a or b must be even.

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A farmer has 200 m of fencing to enclose a rectangular area next to a river (no fence needed on the river side). What dimensions maximize the area?
problems.correct_answer: Width = 50 m, Length = 100 m, Max area = 5000 m²

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: A point moves along the curve y = x³. Find the point where the tangent line has the smallest positive slope.
problems.correct_answer: At x = 0, slope = 0 (not positive). The slope is 3x² which is positive for all x ≠ 0 and approaches 0 as x → 0. There is no smallest positive slope; the infimum is 0 but it is not achieved.
problems.correct_answer_latex: \text{No minimum positive slope exists; inf} = 0 \text{ (not achieved)}

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: Show that the sum of the first n odd numbers equals n².
problems.correct_answer: Proof by induction: Base case n=1: 1 = 1². Inductive step: Assume 1+3+...+(2k-1) = k². Then 1+3+...+(2k-1)+(2k+1) = k² + 2k + 1 = (k+1)². QED.

problems.subject: mixed_descriptive
problems.content: Three circles of radius 1 are mutually tangent. Find the area of the region enclosed between them.
